LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANTS THOMAS DYE, ELY HOERBER, GREG MCCARTHY AND LINCARE INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO EITHER THE EXISTENCE OF AN ADVERSE ACTION BASED ON GENDER OR THAT THE LEGITIMATE BASIS WAS A PRETEXT FOR GENDER DISCRIMINATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ELY HOERBER. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE OTHERWISE DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THROUGH HER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AS TO A DISABILITY ABOUT WHICH SHE INFORMED DEFENDANT, WHICH DEFENDANT DID NOT ACCOMMODATE AND WHICH COULD BE EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT'S LEGITIMATE BASIS WAS PRETEXTUAL. SHE ALSO RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO GENDER HARASSMENT BASED ON HER TESTIMONY CONCERNING DYE'S CONDUCT. MR. KONECKY TO PREPARE FORM OR ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA G. KONECKY OF THE LAW FIRM OF SCHNEIDER, WALLACE, COTTRELL & KONECKY LLP, TEL. NO. 421-7100 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. SUSAN B. BURR OF THE DRINKER BITTLE & REATH LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 591-7639 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS AND MOVING PARTIES.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS THOMAS DYE, ELY HOERBER, GREG MCCARTHY AND LINCARE INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 2 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS NOT RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO EITHER THE EXISTENCE OF AN ADVERSE ACTION BASED ON GENDER OR THAT THE LEGITIMATE BASIS WAS A PRETEXT FOR GENDER DISCRIMINATION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ELY HOERBER. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ARE OTHERWISE DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF DOES RAISE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT THROUGH HER DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AS TO A DISABILITY ABOUT WHICH SHE INFORMED DEFENDANT, WHICH DEFENDANT DID NOT ACCOMMODATE AND WHICH COULD BE EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT'S LEGITIMATE BASIS WAS PRETEXTUAL. SHE ALSO RAISES A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO GENDER HARASSMENT BASED ON HER TESTIMONY CONCERNING DYE'S CONDUCT. MR. KONECKY TO PREPARE FORM OR ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =(301/PJB)

MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: Plaintiff's Motion To Quash Defendants' Subpoenas is granted in part and denied in part. The subpoenas are modified to require production of the records to the court for in camera review to determine the relevance of the records to Plaintiff's claims for treatment and medical restrictions related to her back injury and to her claims for emotional distress. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED (EAH/612)
